abhivādetvā.note: divergence: general dictionaries gloss abhivādeti as "to salute respectfully, pay homage"; the lexicon glosses this occurrence as "having spoken, declared" (from the same root √vad). The lexicon's gloss is followed here.

abhiññātehi.gram: past participle of abhijānāti, instrumental plural

abhiññāya.meaning: with special knowledge, through true understanding; having directly known
abhiññāya.gram: feminine noun abhiññā, instrumental singular; also gerund of abhijānāti
breakdown: Root "to know"; abhiññā = special, direct knowledge.
Root "to see"; abhikkhaṇa contracts to abhiṇha, "repeated, constant".

abhiṇhapaccavekkhitabbaṭhānasuttaṁ.meaning: the Sutta on the Subjects to be Repeatedly Reflected Upon (sutta title)
abhiṇhapaccavekkhitabbaṭhānasuttaṁ.summary: This is a sutta title compounded from abhiṇha "repeatedly", paccavekkhitabba "to be reflected upon", ṭhāna "subject, ground" and sutta "discourse".
breakdown: To be reflected upon, to be reviewed (future passive participle of paccavekkhati).
Root "to see"; abhikkhaṇa contracts to abhiṇha, "repeated, constant".

abhūtaṁ.meaning: untrue, not according to fact; what has not happened
abhūtaṁ.gram: adjective (past participle of na bhavati), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Become, true, factual (past participle of bhavati, √bhū).

abrahmacariyā.meaning: unchastity; conduct contrary to the holy life
abrahmacariyā.summary: The negation of brahmacariya, the chaste "walking on the path of Brahma", i.e. the celibate holy life.
abrahmacariyā.gram: neuter noun, compound; here with ablative ending -ā (abstaining "from unchastity")

abravuṁ:.meaning: they said, they told, they spoke
abravuṁ:.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of brūti (√brū)

abyākata.summary: Used of the questions the Buddha set aside without answering (the "undeclared points").
breakdown: Explained, declared (past participle of byākaroti: vi + ā + √kar).

abyākatāni.gram: past participle of na byākaroti, neuter plural
breakdown: Explained, declared (past participle of byākaroti: vi + ā + √kar).

abyāpajjharatā.meaning: delighting in non-ill will; taking delight in freedom from ill will
breakdown: Delighting in, devoted to (past participle of ramati).

abyāpādasaṅkappo.meaning: thought of non-ill will; intention free from aversion and hatred
abyāpādasaṅkappo.summary: One of the three right thoughts (sammāsaṅkappa) of the Noble Eightfold Path.
abyāpādasaṅkappo.goenka: Abyāpādasaṅkappa, the thought of non-ill will, is one of the three components of sammāsaṅkappa (right thought) in the Noble Eightfold Path, together with thoughts of renunciation (nekkhamma) and non-violence (avihiṁsā). In the Dvedhāvitakka-sutta the Bodhisatta observes that when such thoughts are repeatedly thought and pondered over, the mind becomes predisposed towards them — harming neither oneself nor others, strengthening wisdom and being conducive to nibbāna.

abyāsekasukhaṁ.gram: neuter abstract noun, compound, accusative singular

acalaṭṭhānaṁ.meaning: the unmoving place; the unshakeable sphere
breakdown: Place, sphere, state. (The doubled ṭṭh is sandhi doubling at the compound seam.)

acarī.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of carati (√car)

accayena.meaning: after the lapse of, with the passing of
accayena.gram: masculine noun accaya, instrumental singular, used adverbially
breakdown: Prefix "beyond, past"; before a vowel it becomes acc-.
Root "to go"; accaya = lapse, passing away.

accayo maṁ.meaning: a fault has overcome me (formula of confessing a transgression)
breakdown: Fault, transgression; lapse (masculine noun, nominative singular).

acchariyamanusso.meaning: a wonderful man, an exceptional human being

acchecchi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of chindati (√chid)

acchiddakāritāya.meaning: by faultless performance; through acting without defect
acchiddakāritāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, instrumental singular, compound
breakdown: Negative prefix "not". (The doubled cch is sandhi doubling.)

acchinnavutti.meaning: of impeccable conduct; whose conduct is free of defect
breakdown: Removed, cut off (here: with faults removed).
Prefix "to, on" (with cch doubling by sandhi).
Intensifying prefix "exceedingly"; before a vowel it becomes acc-.
Risen, elevated (past participle of uggacchati, ud + √gam).

acelakā.meaning: naked ascetics (a class of unclothed wanderers)

acintayuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of cinteti (√cint)

breakdown: Entered upon, possessed of (past participle of āpajjati).

adaṇḍāraho.meaning: not deserving punishment; not worthy of the stick

addasā.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of dassati/passati (√dis)

addhānaṁ.meaning: time; a long stretch (of time or road)

adhi.meaning: above, upon, over; in respect of (prefix)

adhiccamidaṁ.meaning: this (arises) without a cause; this is fortuitous
adhiccamidaṁ.summary: A sandhi fusion of adhiccaṁ "without cause, by chance" and idaṁ "this".
breakdown: Without a cause, fortuitously, by chance.
This (neuter demonstrative pronoun). (The ṁ becomes m before a vowel.)

adhicitta.meaning: the higher mind; the meditative mind
adhicitta.summary: Part of the threefold training: adhisīla (higher morality), adhicitta (higher mind, i.e. concentration), adhipaññā (higher wisdom).
adhicitta.goenka: Adhicitta, the higher or meditative mind, names the training of samādhi within the threefold division of the path: adhisīla (higher morality), adhicitta (higher mind) and adhipaññā (higher wisdom). It is the mind developed and unified through meditation, the basis on which experiential wisdom can arise.

adhigacchati.meaning: to acquire, to attain, to find
breakdown: Root "to go" (present stem gaccha-); with adhi-, "to attain, come into possession of".

adhigatavant.meaning: one who has found or attained

adhimuccissati.meaning: will be inclined to, will resolve upon
adhimuccissati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of adhimuccati
breakdown: Root "to release"; adhimuccati = "to incline to, resolve on, be drawn to".

adhipati.meaning: master, ruler; (as adjective) ruled or governed by

adhippāyena.meaning: with the intention, with the thought
breakdown: Root "to go"; adhippāya = intention, wish, sense.
Noun-forming suffix: "a term applied to something".

adhivāsetu.meaning: may (he) consent, comply with, accept, approve
adhivāsetu.gram: verb, 3rd person singular imperative of adhivāseti (causative of adhivasati)
breakdown: Root "to dwell"; causative vāseti — adhivāseti = "to consent, accept (an invitation)".

adhiṭṭheyya.meaning: should practise; should fix one's attention upon, should resolve upon
adhiṭṭheyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of adhiṭṭhāti/adhiṭṭhahati
breakdown: Root "to stand"; adhiṭṭhāti = "to stand firmly on, resolve, fix one's attention".

adhiṭṭhānābhinivesānusayā.meaning: resolutions, clinging inclinations and underlying tendencies
adhiṭṭhānābhinivesānusayā.summary: A three-member compound: adhiṭṭhāna (resolution, determination) + abhinivesa (tendency, inclination, clinging) + anusaya (underlying tendency, predisposition).

adinnampi.meaning: even what is not given; also the not-given
adinnampi.gram: neuter past participle adinna, accusative singular + emphatic particle pi
breakdown: Particle pi "also, even", with junction consonant m.

adinnādānaṁ.meaning: taking what is not given; theft
adinnādānaṁ.summary: A compound of adinna "not given" and ādāna "taking": the second of the unwholesome courses of action, abstention from which is the second precept.
adinnādānaṁ.goenka: Adinnādāna, taking what is not given, is listed among the ten unwholesome actions and is the object of the second precept of sīla: adinnādānā veramaṇī, abstaining from taking what is not given, is wholesome. In the tradition's training, observing this precept is part of the foundation of morality on which samādhi and paññā are built.
